meta.label: Stuffed Grape Leaves (Warak Enab / Dolma)
meta.description: Classic Levantine stuffed grape leaves with a bright lemon finish. Includes a traditional vegetarian rice-herb filling plus an optional meat-and-rice variation.

summary: Levantine stuffed grape leaves (warak enab) are rolled bundles of seasoned rice and herbs simmered gently with lemon. The keys are: rinse/brine-manage jarred leaves, roll snug but not tight (rice expands), pack rolls tightly in the pot so they don’t unravel, and simmer low until the rice is tender. Served warm or room temperature, often with extra lemon and olive oil.

timing.notes: Rolling takes the most time. Total includes simmering and resting.

diet.notes: Vegetarian/vegan version is vegan as written. Optional meat variation is not vegan/vegetarian.

compat.notes: A wide, heavy-bottom pot with a tight lid works best.

foodSafetyNotes: Keep simmer gentle; a hard boil can burst grape leaves and make filling spill.
Cool leftovers quickly and refrigerate within 2 hours.
Reheat gently with a splash of water/stock, or serve chilled/room temperature if preferred.

ingredients.label: Jarred grape leaves
ingredients.notes: 1 large jar (weight varies). Rinse well to remove excess brine.

ingredients.label: Pot liner: sliced potato or tomato
ingredients.notes: Prevents scorching and adds flavor; also helps anchor rolls.

ingredients.label: Olive oil (for pot + finishing)
ingredients.notes: About 3 Tbsp (some goes in pot, some for finishing).

ingredients.label: Lemon, thinly sliced (optional)
ingredients.notes: Optional; layer in pot for extra aroma.

ingredients.label: Short- or medium-grain rice
ingredients.notes: Rinse until water runs mostly clear; do not par-cook for the vegetarian filling.

ingredients.label: Olive oil (for filling)
ingredients.notes: About 1/4 cup; classic filling is generous with olive oil.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt (for filling)
ingredients.notes: Start here; jarred leaves can be salty.

ingredients.label: Water or vegetable stock (cooking liquid)
ingredients.notes: Start here; add more if needed during simmering.

ingredients.label: Salt (optional, cooking liquid)
ingredients.notes: Optional; taste the brine level after rinsing leaves before adding.

ingredients.label: Ground beef or lamb (optional meat variation)
ingredients.notes: Optional. If using meat filling, reduce veggie filling batch or make separate rolls.

ingredients.label: Rice (optional meat variation)
ingredients.notes: Rinsed; mixed with raw ground meat.

steps.title: Prepare the grape leaves
instructions: Separate grape leaves carefully and rinse under cool water to remove brine.
If leaves taste very salty, soak in a bowl of cool water for 10 minutes, then drain.
Sort by size: use medium/large leaves for rolling; reserve torn or small leaves to line the pot.

steps.title: Make the vegetarian rice-herb filling
instructions: In a large bowl, combine rinsed rice, finely diced onion, parsley, dill (optional), mint (optional), diced tomato (optional), lemon juice, olive oil, salt, black pepper, allspice (optional), and cinnamon (optional).
Mix thoroughly. The filling should be glossy and aromatic; it will be raw—do not cook it now.

steps.title: Optional: Make the meat-and-rice filling
instructions: In a separate bowl (if using), combine ground meat, rinsed rice, allspice, cinnamon, salt, and black pepper.
Mix gently until uniform. Keep refrigerated until you roll.

steps.title: Line the pot (prevents sticking)
instructions: Choose a wide pot with a tight lid.
Layer sliced potato or tomato on the bottom, then add a layer of torn/small grape leaves on top.
Optional: add a few lemon slices.

steps.title: Roll the grape leaves
instructions: Work with one leaf at a time. Place leaf shiny-side down (veins up) on a board with the stem end toward you.
If there is a thick stem, trim it flush with the leaf.
Place about 1–2 tsp filling near the stem end (less for small leaves).
Fold the bottom over the filling, fold in both sides, then roll upward into a snug cigar.
Do not roll too tight—rice expands as it cooks.

steps.title: Pack tightly in the pot
instructions: Arrange rolls seam-side down in tight concentric circles.
Make layers as needed, keeping them packed snugly so they don’t unravel.
Drizzle a little olive oil over each layer if desired.

steps.title: Add lemony cooking liquid and weight
instructions: Whisk together water/stock, lemon juice, olive oil, and optional salt.
Pour into the pot until it just reaches the top layer (or slightly below). Avoid flooding too high over the rolls.
Place a heatproof plate (or inverted lid insert) on top of the rolls to keep them submerged and prevent floating/unrolling.

steps.title: Simmer gently until tender
instructions: Bring the pot to a gentle simmer over medium heat (watch closely).
Once simmering, reduce heat to low, cover tightly, and cook 55–75 minutes.
Check a roll at 55 minutes: rice should be tender. Add a splash of water if the pot looks dry during cooking.

steps.title: Rest and absorb
instructions: Turn off heat and let the pot rest (covered) 10–15 minutes to finish steaming and absorb flavors.
Remove the plate. Let cool slightly before serving.

steps.title: Serve
instructions: Serve warm or at room temperature with lemon wedges.
Optional: drizzle with olive oil and serve with yogurt and a cucumber-tomato salad.
Leftovers keep 3–4 days refrigerated; reheat gently with a splash of water or enjoy chilled.

notes: Salt control: Jarred leaves vary widely. Always rinse well and only add extra salt if needed after tasting a small piece of leaf.
Rolling size: Aim for consistent roll size so they cook evenly.
Prevent burning: Keep heat low once simmering; if the pot dries out, add hot water a little at a time along the edge.
Serving style: Many households serve these at room temp with extra lemon; others prefer warm with yogurt.

variations.label: Extra lemony
changes: Increase cooking liquid lemon juice to 90 ml (about 6 Tbsp).
Add more lemon slices between layers.

variations.label: Meat-and-rice only batch
changes: Use the optional meat filling for all rolls.
Simmer gently; test earlier (around 50–60 minutes), since meat filling can cook through while rice finishes.

variations.label: Tomato-forward
changes: Include diced tomato in the filling and line the pot with tomato slices instead of potato.
Use vegetable stock instead of water for cooking liquid.
